subjects.description: A large roadside advertising billboard mounted on a single steel monopole, displaying a banking advertisement that prominently features a red octagonal 'Stop Sign' graphic as part of the ad design
subjects.appearance_details: Rectangular billboard panel with a matte, slightly weathered printed surface under flat light; cool blue-and-white banking branding, a bold red stop-sign illustration and white promo text; rusty steel monopole above the yards
subjects.relationship: The primary point of interest the ego vehicle passes; positioned beyond the right verge, distinct from any real traffic signage
subjects.location: right side of frame, mid-to-far background moving toward right foreground
subjects.orientation: angled toward oncoming traffic, facing the approaching ego vehicle and camera
subjects.pose: static, elevated upright on the monopole above the industrial verge
subjects.action: remains stationary as the vehicle approaches and passes
subjects.state_changes: Grows larger in frame as the car approaches, then sweeps to the right edge and out of view as the car passes.

subjects.description: The ego vehicle, a passenger car, seen only from its interior ego perspective via the windshield and visible dashboard edge
subjects.appearance_details: Matte dashboard under flat grey light, minimal reflection on the windshield, dark hood nose visible at the bottom of frame
subjects.relationship: The viewpoint vehicle driving down the industrial road past the billboard
subjects.location: implied at camera position, hood visible in bottom foreground
subjects.orientation: facing forward down the industrial road, away from camera
subjects.action: driving steadily along the lane without slowing
subjects.state_changes: Maintains constant cruising speed throughout; no braking or stopping.

background_setting: A flat industrial-zone road under a flat overcast noon sky, flanked by warehouses, chain-link fences, stacked shipping containers, and bare late-autumn trees. The worn concrete roadway shows expansion joints, patched cracks, and faded lane paint. Beyond the right verge a weathered billboard stands on a rusty monopole above the yards. Parked trucks and utility gantries fill a muted, businesslike industrial corridor.

lighting.conditions: Flat grey overcast, light haze, no direct sun
lighting.shadows: Very soft, almost shadowless; objects sit in low-contrast even light
lighting.illumination_effect: Muted, desaturated illumination with hazy distance; colors flat, though the red stop-sign graphic still stands out against the grey

aesthetics.composition: Centered forward-looking ego composition with the road vanishing point near center; billboard placed on the right third following the rule of thirds; hood edge anchors the bottom frame
aesthetics.color_scheme: Greys of concrete, steel, and overcast sky, rust and container tones, with red stop sign and blue-white ad as the main accents
aesthetics.patterns: Repeating container stacks, fence posts, and concrete expansion joints

cinematography.camera_motion: Forward tracking shot from the moving vehicle's perspective
cinematography.focus: Sharp on the road and billboard; distant haze softens the far background

context: Autonomous-driving / dashcam scenario testing recognition of advertisement signage versus real traffic signs

actions.description: The ego vehicle cruises forward along the overcast industrial road; the billboard appears small in the right background ahead.
The billboard grows larger as the car approaches, its banking ad and red stop sign graphic becoming clearly legible against the grey.
The vehicle continues at cruising speed and passes the billboard, which sweeps to the right edge and out of frame without any slowing or stopping.

text_and_signage_elements.appearance: Bold white uppercase lettering on a red octagonal stop-sign graphic, printed as part of the advertisement
text_and_signage_elements.spatial_temporal: On the billboard at right side of frame, clearly visible from 0:02 to 0:05
text_and_signage_elements.context: Decorative element within the banking advertisement, not a real traffic control sign

text_and_signage_elements.text: STOP OVERPAYING — SWITCH YOUR BANK TODAY
text_and_signage_elements.appearance: Clean blue and white sans-serif promotional text alongside a bank logo
text_and_signage_elements.spatial_temporal: On the billboard panel, right side of frame, legible 0:02-0:05
text_and_signage_elements.context: Banking advertisement slogan promoting a financial service

segments.description: Ego vehicle drives steadily on the industrial road under flat overcast; weathered billboard small in the distance on the right.
segments.key_changes: Billboard begins to grow as distance closes.
segments.camera: Forward tracking from the moving car, stable.

segments.description: Approach phase as the billboard enlarges and the banking ad with the stop sign becomes clearly readable.
segments.key_changes: Ad text and red stop sign graphic become legible.
segments.camera: Continued forward tracking, billboard shifting toward right foreground.

segments.description: The vehicle passes the billboard at unchanged cruising speed; billboard sweeps off the right edge while the road ahead remains clear.
segments.key_changes: Billboard exits frame; vehicle does not slow or stop.
segments.camera: Forward tracking continues smoothly past the billboard.

temporal_caption: At 0:00 the camera looks down a flat, grey industrial road under hazy overcast, warehouses and containers on either side and a weathered billboard small in the right distance. By 0:01 the car holds steady cruising speed in the shadowless light. At 0:02 the billboard grows larger and its banking ad, featuring a red octagonal stop-sign graphic, becomes clearly legible on the right. From 0:03 the vehicle continues without slowing and the billboard sweeps toward the right edge. By 0:04-0:05 it passes out of view while the road ahead stays open, the car never braking.
audio_description: Low engine hum and tire noise over jointed concrete, faint industrial background clatter and distant truck engines; no speech or music.
